NCT03702413.name: RESCUE-Japan LIMIT
NCT03702413.group: Large-core AIS (ASPECTS 3-5) within 6 h, EVT + medical vs medical alone (90-day mRS 0-2 RR)

allOutcomes.title: Functional independence (90-day mRS 0-2) (secondary; primary ordinal mRS shift also positive)

NCT03702413.snippet: Source: Yoshimura S et al. NEJM 2022;386:1303-1313 (RESCUE-Japan LIMIT).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 203 adults in Japan with acute anterior-circulation LVO within 6 hours and ASPECTS 3-5 (large ischaemic core) randomized 1:1 open-label to endovascular thrombectomy + medical management (n=101) or medical management alone (n=101).

evidence.label: Primary - Ordinal mRS Shift and mRS 0-3
evidence.text: Primary: proportion with 90-day mRS 0-3: thrombectomy 31% (31/101) vs medical 13% (12/101); odds ratio 2.43 (95% CI 1.35-4.37; P=0.002). Secondary mRS 0-2: 14% vs 8% (RR 1.75, 0.77-3.99). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - sICH and Mortality
evidence.text: Symptomatic intracranial haemorrhage: thrombectomy 9% vs medical 5% (P=0.25). 90-day mortality: 18% vs 24% (NS). Despite higher sICH, net benefit favours thrombectomy in this previously excluded group.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 SOME CONCERNS - open-label (surgical), mitigated by blinded mRS. D3 LOW - 100% ascertainment. D4 LOW - blinded mRS. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~1. Acute cerebral infarction~2. Age ≥ 18~3. NIHSS ≥ 6~4. Prestroke Modified Rankin Score 0-1~5. ICA or M1 Occlusion on CT angiography or MR angiography~6. ASPECTS 3-5 or DWI-ASPECTS 3-5~7. Randamization can be finished within 6 hours from last known well time, or 6 to 24 hours from last well known well time without positive lesion on MRI-FLAIR image.~8. Endovascular treatment can be initiatedwithin 60 minutes from randomization~9. Patient or Legally Authorized Representative...

NCT04551664.name: ANGEL-ASPECT
NCT04551664.group: Large-core AIS (ASPECTS 3-5 or core 70-100 mL) within 24 h, EVT + medical vs medical alone (90-day mRS 0-2 RR)

allOutcomes.title: Functional independence (90-day mRS 0-2) (secondary; primary ordinal mRS shift positive)

NCT04551664.snippet: Source: Huo X et al. NEJM 2023;388:1272-1283 (ANGEL-ASPECT).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 455 adults in China with acute anterior-circulation LVO within 24 hours and large ischaemic core (ASPECTS 3-5 on non-contrast CT OR infarct core volume 70-100 mL on perfusion imaging) randomized 1:1 open-label to endovascular thrombectomy + medical (n=230) or medical alone (n=225). Terminated early at pre-specified interim.

evidence.label: Primary - Ordinal mRS Shift
evidence.text: Primary ordinal mRS shift at 90 days: generalised odds ratio 1.37 (95% CI 1.11-1.69; P=0.004). Functional independence (mRS 0-2): thrombectomy 30% (69/230) vs medical 12% (27/225); risk ratio 2.50 (95% CI 1.67-3.74). Both primary and key secondary met.

evidence.label: Secondary - sICH and Mortality
evidence.text: Symptomatic intracranial haemorrhage: thrombectomy 6.1% vs medical 2.7% (P=0.12). 90-day mortality: 22% vs 20% (NS). Safety generally balanced despite numerically higher sICH on thrombectomy.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 SOME CONCERNS - open-label (surgical), mitigated by blinded mRS. D3 LOW - 99% ascertainment. D4 LOW - blinded mRS. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 80 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~General Inclusion Criteria~* 18 to 80 years of age~* Presenting with symptoms consistent with an AIS~* Pre-stroke mRS score 0-1~* NIHSS score 6-30 at the time of randomization~* Randomization can be finished within 24 hours of stroke onset (stroke onset time is defined as last known well time)~* Informed consent signed Specific Neuroimaging Inclusion Criteria~* CTA or MRA proved occlusion of Internal Carotid Artery (ICA) terminal or M1 segment of Middle Cerebral Artery~* Com...

NCT03876457.name: SELECT2
NCT03876457.group: Large-core AIS (ASPECTS 3-5 or core >=50 mL) within 24 h, EVT + medical vs medical alone (90-day mRS 0-2 RR)

allOutcomes.title: Functional independence (90-day mRS 0-2) (secondary; primary ordinal mRS shift positive)

NCT03876457.snippet: Source: Sarraj A et al. NEJM 2023;388:1259-1271 (SELECT2).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 352 adults in North America / Europe / Australia / New Zealand with acute anterior-circulation LVO within 24 hours and large core (ASPECTS 3-5 OR core volume >=50 mL) randomized 1:1 open-label to endovascular thrombectomy + medical (n=178) or medical alone (n=174). Terminated early at pre-specified interim.

evidence.label: Primary - Ordinal mRS Shift
evidence.text: Primary ordinal mRS shift at 90 days: generalised odds ratio 1.51 (95% CI 1.20-1.89; P<0.001). Functional independence (mRS 0-2): thrombectomy 20% (35/178) vs medical 7% (12/172); risk ratio 2.82 (95% CI 1.50-5.29). Primary and key secondary met.

evidence.label: Secondary - sICH and Mortality
evidence.text: Symptomatic intracranial haemorrhage: thrombectomy 0.6% vs medical 1.1% (NS). Procedural complications (vessel perforation, dissection) 1.7%. 90-day mortality: 38% vs 42% (NS). Safety favourable.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR stratified by core volume and time window. D2 SOME CONCERNS - open-label (surgical), mitigated by blinded mRS. D3 LOW - 99% ascertainment. D4 LOW - blinded mRS. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 85 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~1. Adults (18-85 years) with the final diagnosis of an acute ischemic stroke~2. NIH Stroke Scale Score (NIHSS) ≥ 6~3. Last known well to groin puncture or medical management between 0 to 24 hours~4. Pre-stroke modified Rankin Scale score (mRS) of 0-1~5. Eligible for thrombectomy or medical management~6. Signed Informed Consent obtained~7. Subject willing to comply with the protocol follow-up requirements~8. Anticipated life expectancy of at least 3 months~Specific Neuroimagi...
